drawer.title: Page Quality Audit
drawer.stale: The page has been modified since this audit - the results below may be outdated. Re-run when you are done editing.

eco.disclaimer: Page-level checks against the RGESN 2024 Frontend/Contents families. This is not an RGESN conformity score - a full assessment covers the whole service (hosting, backend, governance); see the manual checklist below. Resource figures are from the editing-workspace render and are indicative.

pageWeight.title: Heavy page ({{size}})
pageWeight.detail: Compress images, drop unused scripts/styles and defer non-critical resources. A lighter page consumes less energy and bandwidth per visit (RGESN Frontend).

requests.title: {{count}} HTTP requests
requests.detail: Each request costs a round-trip and server work. Combine assets, lazy-load below-the-fold content and remove unused third-party scripts (RGESN Frontend).

domSize.title: Large DOM ({{count}} nodes)
domSize.detail: A heavy DOM increases memory use and rendering energy on the visitor's device. Simplify the markup or paginate very long pages (RGESN Frontend).

lazyLoading.title: {{count}} below-the-fold images load eagerly
lazyLoading.detail: Add loading="lazy" so off-screen images are only fetched if the visitor scrolls to them - avoids downloading data that is never seen (RGESN Frontend).

imageFormats.title: {{count}} heavy images in legacy formats
imageFormats.detail: Serve WebP or AVIF instead of JPEG/PNG for a large size reduction at equal quality; let Jahia generate modern-format renditions (RGESN Contents).

oversizedImages.title: {{count}} images larger than displayed
oversizedImages.detail: The source file is much bigger than its rendered size. Upload resized variants or use responsive renditions so no wasted pixels are transferred (RGESN Contents).

imageDimensions.title: {{count}} images without width/height
imageDimensions.detail: Explicit dimensions avoid re-layouts (and the extra rendering work they cost) as images load (RGESN Contents).

webFonts.title: {{count}} web font files loaded
webFonts.detail: Each font file is an extra download. Prefer system fonts, limit weights/styles, and use WOFF2 only (RGESN Frontend).

autoplay.title: {{count}} media elements autoplay
autoplay.detail: Autoplaying video/audio consumes bandwidth and battery before any interaction. Require an explicit play action (RGESN UX/UI).

thirdParty.title: {{count}} third-party origins
thirdParty.detail: Each external origin adds DNS/TLS connections, energy and privacy exposure. Self-host or remove non-essential third parties (RGESN Architecture).

checklist.title: Manual checklist (whole-service, not automatable)
checklist.intro: These RGESN criteria are assessed at the service level and cannot be judged from a single page. Review them with the team responsible for the platform.

envTarget.title: Environmental targets are defined and owned
envTarget.detail: The service has documented eco-design objectives and a person accountable for them (RGESN Strategy).

reviewMissing.title: Environmental impact is measured and reviewed
reviewMissing.detail: Impact indicators are tracked over releases, not set once and forgotten (RGESN Strategy).

greenHost.title: Low-carbon hosting
greenHost.detail: The infrastructure runs on a provider with a documented low PUE and renewable-energy commitment (RGESN Hosting).

caching.title: Effective caching of static resources
caching.detail: Long-lived cache headers and a CDN avoid re-downloading unchanged assets on every visit (RGESN Hosting/Architecture).

apiEfficiency.title: APIs return only what is needed
apiEfficiency.detail: Endpoints avoid over-fetching; payloads are paginated and scoped to the fields actually used (RGESN Backend).

pagination.title: Pagination over infinite scroll for large lists
pagination.detail: Infinite scroll keeps loading data the visitor may never read; paginate or lazy-load on demand (RGESN Backend/UX).

featureNeed.title: Every feature earns its footprint
featureNeed.detail: Features are justified against user need; unused ones are removed rather than shipped and maintained (RGESN UX).

noDarkPattern.title: No engagement dark patterns
noDarkPattern.detail: Autoplay carousels, aggressive notifications and endless feeds drive needless traffic and energy - avoid them (RGESN UX).

ai.notConfigured: AI review is not configured on this server. An administrator must set AI_PROVIDER, AI_MODEL and AI_API_KEY in:
ai.intro: Sends the page text and the audit findings to {{provider}} ({{model}}) and returns prioritized, editor-friendly recommendations. The API key stays on the server.
ai.generating: Analyzing the page with AI… this takes a few seconds.
ai.truncated: The answer was cut short by the AI_MAX_TOKENS limit - some recommendations may be missing. Ask an administrator to raise AI_MAX_TOKENS in the module configuration.
ai.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions: review before acting on them.

jahia.rawKeys: Raw translation keys visible on the page

rawI18nKeys.title: {{count}} raw translation keys visible to visitors
rawI18nKeys.detail: Text like namespace:key.path means a resource bundle or locale JSON entry is missing: visitors see the technical key instead of the label. Usually a module i18n gap.

unpublishedChanges.title: {{count}} content blocks not published
unpublishedChanges.detail: These nodes were modified or never published: visitors currently see an older version of this page, or nothing at all. Publish the page to align live with what you are editing.

missingTranslations.title: {{count}} content blocks missing translations ({{languages}})
missingTranslations.detail: This content renders empty or falls back to another language for visitors browsing in those languages. Open each node in the editor and fill in the missing languages.

placeholderText.title: {{count}} placeholder texts on the page
placeholderText.detail: Lorem ipsum / TODO text is still visible. Replace it with final content before publishing.

graphqlUnavailable.title: Repository checks could not run
graphqlUnavailable.detail: The GraphQL query failed, so publication and translation checks were skipped. The on-page checks above remain valid.

seo.noPreview: No title or og:title found - nothing to preview.
seo.noOgImage: No og:image - shared links will have no visual

noTitle.title: The page has no title
noTitle.detail: The <title> is the strongest on-page SEO signal and the text shown in search results. Set one in the page's SEO properties.

titleLength.title: Title is {{length}} characters
titleLength.detail: Aim for {{min}}-{{max}} characters: shorter wastes the space, longer gets truncated in search results.

noDescription.title: No meta description
noDescription.detail: Search engines will pick an arbitrary text snippet. Write a 50-160 character summary that makes people want to click.

descriptionLength.title: Meta description is {{length}} characters
descriptionLength.detail: Aim for {{min}}-{{max}} characters so the full text shows in search results.

noindex.title: Page is excluded from search engines (noindex)
noindex.detail: The robots meta tag contains noindex ({{content}}). If this page should be public, remove it - it will never appear in search results.

nofollow.title: Links are not followed (nofollow)
nofollow.detail: The robots meta tag contains nofollow ({{content}}): search engines ignore every link on this page. Verify it is intentional.

noCanonical.title: No canonical URL
noCanonical.detail: A canonical link prevents duplicate-content issues when the page is reachable through several URLs (vanity URLs, parameters).

incompleteOg.title: Incomplete Open Graph tags
incompleteOg.detail: Without og:title and og:image, links shared on social networks and chat tools render as bare URLs. Fill in the page's social/SEO properties.

invalidStructuredData.title: {{count}} invalid JSON-LD blocks
invalidStructuredData.detail: The structured data does not parse, so search engines ignore it. Validate the JSON-LD markup.

noStructuredData.title: No structured data
noStructuredData.detail: JSON-LD (Article, Organization, BreadcrumbList…) enables rich results in search engines. Worth adding on key pages.

noLang.title: Missing lang attribute on <html>
noLang.detail: Screen readers and search engines cannot tell what language the page is in. The template should set <html lang>.

langMismatch.title: Page declares lang="{{htmlLang}}" but is audited in "{{expected}}"
langMismatch.detail: A wrong lang attribute confuses search engines and screen reader pronunciation. Check the template's language handling.

missingAlt.title: {{count}} images without alt attribute
missingAlt.detail: Alt text is how search engines and screen readers understand images. Fill the alt field on each image in the content editor.

genericAnchors.title: {{count}} generic link texts ("click here", "read more"…)
genericAnchors.detail: Descriptive link text helps SEO and screen reader users. Say where the link goes: "Read the 2026 annual report".

links.externalNote: external links are not verified (browser cross-origin restriction)

brokenLinks.title: {{count}} broken internal links
brokenLinks.detail: These links return an error for visitors. Fix or remove them - the list below shows each one with a highlight button.

hardcodedUrls.title: {{count}} absolute URLs hardcoded to this host
hardcodedUrls.detail: Links written with the full http(s)://host break when the site moves environment or domain. Use internal page links instead.

mixedContent.title: {{count}} http:// links on an https page
mixedContent.detail: Browsers flag or block mixed content. Switch these URLs to https.

blankNoopener.title: {{count}} target="_blank" links without rel="noopener"
blankNoopener.detail: Pages opened in a new tab get access to this page's window object. Add rel="noopener" in the template or rich-text content.

recs.title: Recommendations
recs.none: Nothing to improve in this category.

a11y.noViolations: No violations detected by automated checks.
a11y.machineNote: Automated checks cover a subset of WCAG criteria. A clean result does not certify full compliance; the manual checklist below covers the rest.
a11y.incompleteNote: The engine suspects an issue here but cannot decide automatically - review each case.

checklist.title: Manual checklist (not automatable)
checklist.intro: These WCAG criteria require human judgment - no tool can verify them. Review them once per page type or template.

keyboard.title: Everything operable with the keyboard alone
keyboard.detail: Tab / Shift+Tab through the page: every menu, carousel, accordion and dialog must be reachable and usable, with no focus trap (WCAG 2.1.1, 2.1.2).

altQuality.title: Alt texts are meaningful
altQuality.detail: The tool checks that alt exists - only you can judge if it describes the image. Decorative images should have an empty alt (WCAG 1.1.1).

media.title: Videos have captions, audio has transcripts
media.detail: And the captions are accurate, not just auto-generated noise (WCAG 1.2.1-1.2.3).

focusOrder.title: Focus and reading order match the visual order
focusOrder.detail: Tab through the page: the focus must move in the order a sighted reader would scan it (WCAG 1.3.2, 2.4.3).

linkPurpose.title: Every link makes sense out of context
linkPurpose.detail: A screen reader user often lists all links: each label alone must say where it leads (WCAG 2.4.4).

zoom.title: Page usable at 200% zoom
zoom.detail: Ctrl/Cmd + until 200%: no text loss, no overlapping content (WCAG 1.4.4).

reflow.title: Content reflows at 320 px width
reflow.detail: Narrow the window (or use the mobile preview): no horizontal scrolling of the page body (WCAG 1.4.10).

focusVisible.title: Keyboard focus is always visible
focusVisible.detail: While tabbing, you must always see where you are - no invisible focus states (WCAG 2.4.7).

formErrors.title: Form errors are explained and fixable
formErrors.detail: Errors are identified in text (not color alone) and suggest a correction (WCAG 3.3.1, 3.3.3).

consistency.title: Navigation and components consistent across pages
consistency.detail: Menus, search, breadcrumbs appear in the same place and behave the same on every page (WCAG 3.2.3, 3.2.4).

readingLevel.title: Text understandable at lower-secondary reading level
readingLevel.detail: Use the Readability tab as a proxy, then judge jargon and sentence complexity yourself (WCAG 3.1.5).

timing.title: No time limits, or they can be turned off
timing.detail: Sessions, carousels or forms must not expire without warning and control (WCAG 2.2.3).

targetSize.title: Touch targets at least 44×44 px
targetSize.detail: Buttons and links comfortably tappable on mobile, with spacing between them (WCAG 2.5.5).

contextHelp.title: Context-sensitive help available
contextHelp.detail: Complex forms offer inline help or instructions where the user needs them (WCAG 3.3.5).

vitals.labNote: Lab measurement of this preview render. Values are directional and differ from real-user (field) data.

slowLcp.title: Main content renders slowly
slowLcp.detail: Compress or preload the hero image / largest block so visitors see the main content sooner.

highCls.title: Layout shifts while loading
highCls.detail: Reserve space for images, embeds and late-loading blocks so the page does not jump under the reader.

slowTtfb.title: Slow server response (TTFB)
slowTtfb.detail: The HTML itself is slow to arrive: enable page caching or review server load before optimizing anything else.

missingDims.title: {{count}} images without width/height attributes
missingDims.detail: Add width and height so the browser reserves the space up front - this is the most common cause of layout shifts.

oversizedImages.title: {{count}} images heavier than needed
oversizedImages.detail: The source file is more than twice its displayed size. Upload resized variants or let Jahia serve smaller renditions.

heavyPage.title: Heavy page ({{size}})
heavyPage.detail: Compress images and remove unused scripts/styles to reduce total download weight, especially for mobile visitors.

manyRequests.title: {{count}} network requests
manyRequests.detail: Combine or drop resources and lazy-load below-the-fold content: each request adds latency.

largeDom.title: Large DOM ({{count}} nodes)
largeDom.detail: Simplify the markup or split very long pages: a large DOM slows rendering and every interaction.

heavyAssets.title: {{count}} very large files (e.g. {{name}})
heavyAssets.detail: Minify, compress or split these assets, or load them only where actually used.

readability.h1Hint: a page should have exactly one H1
readability.headingSkipsHint: avoid jumping levels (e.g. H2 to H4)
readability.noText: Not enough text on this page to compute readability ({{count}} words found).

veryHardText.title: Text is very difficult to read (score {{score}})
veryHardText.detail: Shorten sentences and prefer everyday words. Aim for a score above 50 for a general audience.

hardText.title: Text is difficult to read (score {{score}})
hardText.detail: Shorten the longest sentences and replace jargon: a score of 50-70 suits most website visitors.

longSentences.title: {{count}} sentences longer than 25 words
longSentences.detail: Split them - one idea per sentence. Long sentences are the main driver of a poor readability score.

longParagraphs.title: Dense paragraphs ({{count}} words on average)
longParagraphs.detail: Break paragraphs up: 40-60 words per paragraph reads comfortably on screen.

h1Count.title: {{count}} H1 headings on the page
h1Count.detail: Use exactly one H1: it anchors the page for readers, screen readers and search engines.

headingSkips.title: {{count}} skipped heading levels
headingSkips.detail: Do not jump levels (e.g. H2 straight to H4): fix the hierarchy so the document outline stays meaningful.

thinContent.title: Little text on the page ({{count}} words)
thinContent.detail: Pages under ~300 words inform and rank poorly. Consider expanding the content or merging it elsewhere.
